latest.title: Lions hunt better when the pride coordinates
latest.script_key: lions turn hunting into a team problem watch the spacing before the chase because lions can use different positions in a group hunt one animal pressures while another blocks the escape path the power is not just muscle which predator tactic should we break down next

latest.title: Mountain rivers carve canyons faster than you think
latest.script_key: this river eats rock alive see how the water curves the walls smooth that s the rock itself dissolving minute by minute because the water s slightly acidic that s why the canyon keeps widening ever wonder how fast it s about a paper thin slice every year

latest.title: Magnets make invisible fields visible
latest.script_key: magnets can show a hidden force map watch the filings line up because each tiny piece becomes a small magnet in the field together they trace the direction of the force around the magnet the pattern is physics drawing itself which force next

latest.title: Dragonfly eyes see way more than yours
latest.script_key: eyes built for snipers look at those bulging compound eyes each one s a mosaic of 30 000 lenses that s why they lock onto prey mid air without blinking they see ultraviolet too so flowers glow for them did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Lions use dark ear marks to guide cubs
latest.script_key: lions have dark ear marks cubs can follow watch the backs of the ears because those dark marks can stand out when a lion moves through grass for cubs that contrast helps track the adult ahead the detail is small but the pride depends on staying together would you spot it

latest.title: Forests make cooler air under the canopy
latest.script_key: forests can cool the air below them watch the shade and mist because leaves block direct sun and release water vapor that combination can make the forest floor feel like a different climate the canopy is a cooling machine not just cover which forest layer next

latest.title: Fire didn't carve these mountains
latest.script_key: fire didn t carve these peaks look close the black streaks are just soot not the real shape that s why the jagged ridges were here first because glaciers tore them down over thousands of years that ice ground the rock into these sharp spines did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Eucalyptus trees save koalas from starvation
latest.script_key: koalas only eat this leaf that s why they pick certain eucalyptus trees over others because the rest taste bitter watch this one twice look how the koala turns the leaf like a tiny salad spinner that s not cute that s why they get more juice out of the juiciest leaf would you survive on a eucalyptus diet

latest.title: Coral reefs are cities built by tiny animals
latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: Dogs read human pointing surprisingly well
latest.script_key: dogs can follow a human point fast watch the eyes check the person because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures that skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated the small glance is social intelligence not random obedience what dog skill should come next

latest.title: Rock layers store ancient environments in stripes
latest.script_key: rock layers are time stamps made of stone watch the stripe pattern because each layer can mark a different setting river mud ocean floor windblown sand or volcanic ash stack enough layers and the cliff becomes a timeline which rock clue should we read next

latest.title: pink flowers don't smell the same to everyone
latest.script_key: this pink flower lies to your nose what you smell depends on your genes some people get a sweet vanilla others get nothing at all that s why a bouquet feels different in every room want to test it sniff a pink carnation right now and tell me what you get

latest.title: Auroras glow when solar particles hit air
latest.script_key: auroras start with charged particles from the sun watch the moving color because charged particles follow earth s magnetic field toward the upper atmosphere when they hit oxygen and nitrogen the air releases light the sky is reacting to space weather which rare sky event next

latest.title: Trees grow underground just like they do above
latest.script_key: trees don t just stand still watch her feet those roots are moving they stretch out to drink not just to hold on roots grow their own tiny soil tunnels that s why like a subway under your feet ever wondered how a tree finds water underground watch this one twice

latest.title: Seals track fish trails with whiskers
latest.script_key: seals can follow wakes with their whiskers watch the whiskers near the water because seals can sense tiny trails left by moving fish those vibrations help them hunt even when the water is dark or messy the face becomes a current detector which ocean sense next

latest.title: Sharks sense tiny electric fields
latest.script_key: sharks can feel electricity in the water watch the head before the turn because sharks have sensory pores that detect tiny electric fields from other animals that helps them hunt when sight is limited the ocean is full of signals we cannot feel would you swim differently

latest.title: horses sense danger faster than you think
latest.script_key: watch those ears swivel toward the tree that s not just curiosity horses rotate each ear independently to pinpoint sounds one ear faces forward the other back that s why they react before we even hear it so next time you re around horses listen to them instead of looking around

latest.title: Lightning turns air into a shock wave
latest.script_key: lightning turns air into a shock wave watch the flash first because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast that air expands suddenly and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder the sound is the atmosphere snapping back which storm signal next

latest.title: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal weather from above the clouds
latest.script_key: storm clouds look different when seen from above watch the cloud tops because the upper shape shows where air is rising flattening or spreading out from above weather becomes a texture map instead of a ceiling which above cloud clue should we compare next

latest.title: Monkeys wash food when grit gets annoying
latest.script_key: monkeys can change habits to solve small problems watch the hands near the food because some monkeys learn useful routines from their group washing or handling food differently can remove grit and make eating easier the habit spreads because it works which primate trick should come next
